data.title: Americas Gold and Silver: Strong Q2 Beat, Operational Momentum and Deleveraging Support Buy Rating and Unchanged $12 Target
data.description:
Analyst Brian Quast of BMO Capital reiterated a Buy rating on Americas Gold and Silver, retaining the price target of C$12.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Brian Quast has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including stronger-than-expected operating performance and reaffirmed guidance. Americas Gold and Silver delivered second-quarter silver output that exceeded both his and the market’s forecasts, with both Cosalá and Galena performing above plan, while production of copper, lead, and antimony also surpassed expectations.

Brian Quast’s rating is based on his view that the company can still meet its full-year production targets, supported by a planned ramp-up at Galena and recent infrastructure upgrades that should reduce operational disruptions. He also highlights that inventory builds and the removal of over US$85 million in future variable debt obligations improve the company’s financial flexibility, and with execution of its optimization strategy, he sees meaningful upside potential, justifying an unchanged $12 target price and a Buy recommendation.

In another report released today, H.C. Wainwright also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $9.75 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Analyst Reaffirms Buy on Choice Properties, Citing Solid FFO, NOI Growth and Strategic $5B First Capital Acquisition
data.description:
Choice Properties Real Estate Investment, the Real Estate sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst yesterday. Analyst Tom Callaghan from BMO Capital maintained a Buy rating on the stock and has a C$17.00 price target.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Tom Callaghan has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that underscore both earnings visibility and balance sheet flexibility. He notes that Choice Properties’ recent quarter showed steady funds-from-operations and solid same-property NOI growth, particularly from the industrial segment, while management reaffirmed its 2026 growth targets, indicating confidence in the medium-term outlook.

He also highlights the strategic rationale of the planned $5.0B First Capital asset acquisition, which should improve the quality and growth profile of the retail portfolio and support valuation multiple expansion over time. Although leverage is expected to rise following the transaction, he views this as manageable given the REIT’s historically conservative capital structure, proven deleveraging track record, and a valuation that, while toward the higher end of its range, is justified by a strong retail environment, a sizable high-quality portfolio, and capable management.

PPRQF’s price has also changed slightly for the past six months – from C$15.700 to C$15.780, which is a 0.51% increase.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Analyst Maintains Hold on Edwards with Neutral Risk-Reward and Reiterated $85 Price Target Amid Pending TAVR Catalysts
data.description:
Canaccord Genuity analyst William Plovanic maintained a Hold rating on Edwards Lifesciences yesterday and set a price target of $85.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

William Plovanic has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors tied to Edwards’ current performance and its risk‑reward profile. The company delivered a strong quarterly beat in both TAVR and TMTT, and management raised full‑year revenue guidance, but the increase largely mirrors the recent upside rather than signaling a fundamentally higher growth trajectory.

At the same time, key catalysts for the core TAVR business, including the final CMS coverage decision and the PROGRESS trial outcome, are still pending and could materially influence the long‑term outlook. Management’s cautious stance on the second half of the year, tougher TAVR comparisons, FX‑driven margin pressure, and a valuation that already discounts much of the expected growth collectively support maintaining a neutral, Hold recommendation and an $85 price target.

According to TipRanks, Plovanic is a 2-star analyst with an average return of 0.0% and a 40.88% success rate. Plovanic covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as TransMedics Group, Atricure, and Artivion.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Bullish Buy Rating Reiterated as Tokenization Strategy and Stablecoin Growth Support Unchanged $50 Price Target
data.description:
Analyst Joseph Vafi from Canaccord Genuity maintained a Buy rating on Bullish and keeping the price target at $50.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Joseph Vafi has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that support a constructive outlook on Bullish. He notes that while quarterly trading volumes fluctuated with broader crypto volatility, key profitability metrics such as average trading spreads showed steady improvement, indicating healthier unit economics and resilient platform performance.

Vafi also emphasizes Bullish’s strategic positioning in the emerging asset tokenization space, particularly through its Equiniti transaction, which he views as aligning the company with future infrastructure needs. He highlights growing stablecoin usage, advancing U.S. legislative efforts such as the anticipated CLARITY Act, and recently improving crypto ETF inflows as catalysts that could drive higher activity on Bullish’s platform, supporting his unchanged $50 price target and Buy recommendation.

In another report released today, Clear Street also assigned a Buy rating to the stock with a $40.00 price target.
